summary: The changes introduce a new retry mechanism for API calls and update error handling in the authentication module. Overall quality is good with a few areas for improvement.

findings.title: Unbounded retry loop on 429 responses
findings.description: The retry loop has no maximum attempt limit. A persistent 429 from the server will cause the process to hang indefinitely.
findings.suggestion: Add a max_retries constant (e.g., 5) and break out of the loop after exceeding it, returning ReviewError::Api.

findings.title: Env var read not wrapped in zeroizing guard
findings.description: The plaintext API key from std::env::var remains in memory after use. If the process dumps core, the key could be exposed.
findings.suggestion: Wrap the returned String in zeroize::Zeroizing<String> so it is scrubbed on drop.

findings.title: Magic number for default max_concurrent
findings.description: The value 4 is used directly without a named constant, making it harder to discover and change.
findings.suggestion: Extract to a const DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENT: usize = 4 at module level.

findings.title: Good use of nonce-misuse resistant cipher
findings.description: AES-256-GCM-SIV is an excellent choice for this use case -- it tolerates accidental nonce reuse without catastrophic failure.
